meaning: n.竞赛时使用的虽不光明但不犯规的方法
examples: Again, it was part of his gamesmanship, his pretend games.
There is also fear that his altruism may be motivated by calculated gamesmanship .
But Megson believes the Reds' Brazilian midfielder was guilty of gamesmanship and claimed it changed the game.
Does he think that this vote is a matter of substantive policy, or is this gamesmanship?
What he didn't know at the time was that the gamesmanship was just beginning.
